description:
You are a build engineer who was handed a one-week capture of every shared library that was staged during nightly builds.  
 The capture is stored at:

   /home/user/build/raw_artifacts.log

 Every line of that file is the filename of a single shared library (e.g. “libfoo.so”).  
 Your goal is to determine how many times each library was staged.

 1. Read /home/user/build/raw_artifacts.log.  
 2. Produce a frequency-sorted report that lists, on one line per library,
       COUNT␣FILENAME
    where:
       • COUNT is the exact decimal frequency (no leading spaces, no zero-padding).  
       • There is exactly one ASCII space character between COUNT and FILENAME.  
       • The list must be sorted first by COUNT in descending numeric order and,
         for ties, alphabetically by FILENAME (ascending).  
 3. Save the report to

       /home/user/build/artifact_frequency.log

 4. After creating the file, print its contents to stdout so that anyone looking at
    the console can immediately verify the result.

 Hints (do NOT print these commands verbatim):
 • You can accomplish everything with a pipeline that involves “sort” and “uniq -c”.
 • “awk” or “sed” can strip the leading spaces that “uniq -c” includes in its output.
 • A second “sort” can give you the desired final ordering.

 The automated test will confirm that /home/user/build/artifact_frequency.log
 exists, is non-empty, and its exact contents match the format and order
 described above.
truth:
Initial files present when the scenario starts:

   1. Directory: /home/user/build
      Permissions: drwxr-xr-x (owned by user:user)

   2. File: /home/user/build/raw_artifacts.log
      Permissions: -rw-r--r-- (owned by user:user)
      Exact byte content (7 lines, LF line endings):

        libfoo.so
        libbar.so
        libfoo.so
        libbaz.so
        libfoo.so
        libbar.so
        libqux.so

 Expected file that must be created by the agent:

   1. File: /home/user/build/artifact_frequency.log
      Permissions: -rw-r--r-- (owned by user:user)
      Exact byte content (4 lines, LF line endings):

        3 libfoo.so
        2 libbar.so
        1 libbaz.so
        1 libqux.so
